reference_answer: Patrick S. Pacious. (Item 5.02, first paragraph.) Note: the document writes "President & Chief Executive Officer" with an ampersand, not "and." Other sections refer to him as "Mr. Pacious" without title.

Dominic E. Dragisich, immediately prior role: Chief Growth & Strategy Officer (effective from March 24, 2026 through May 20, 2026). The document is explicit: "the Board appointed Dominic E. Dragisich, the Company's Chief Growth & Strategy Officer, as the Company's Interim CEO."
915 Meeting Street, Suite 600, North Bethesda, Maryland 20852. (Cover-page XBRL-style bullet block.)
44. ("Mr. Dragisich, age 44, has served as Chief Growth & Strategy Officer since March 24, 2026.")

$500,000, payable on December 31, 2026, subject to his continued employment through that date. Plus a separate time-vesting restricted stock unit award valued at $500,000 that vests on the one-year anniversary of grant.

Item 5.02 ("Departure of Directors or Certain Officers; Election of Directors; Appointment of Certain Officers; Compensatory Arrangements of Certain Officers"). This is the only substantive Item — there is no separate Item 1.01 or Item 7.01 in this 8-K. The press release is referenced inside the Item 5.02 narrative, and Exhibit 99.1 is listed under Item 9.01.

The reference appears in the lead-in to the bullet-list of Pacious's severance benefits: "payments and benefits payable under his previously agreed to and disclosed Severance Benefit Agreement, as amended, effective May 24, 2022." The current Separation Agreement is stated to be "substantially the same as" the 2022 agreement. The full 2022 agreement is not attached to this 8-K.

In the second of the five severance bullets, parenthetically: '"... a two-year period commencing on August 31, 2026, or such earlier date that Mr. Pacious's employment with the Company actually terminates (the "Separation Date")."' The Separation Date is therefore the later of (a) August 31, 2026 [no — actually, the earlier of (a) August 31, 2026 and (b) any earlier termination date]. The bullet uses "commencing on August 31, 2026, or such earlier date" — meaning the Separation Date is fixed as August 31, 2026 unless Pacious's employment terminates earlier.

Exhibit 10.1. The qualification is the standard SEC formulation: "The foregoing summary description of the terms of the Separation Agreement does not purport to be complete and is qualified in its entirety by reference to the Separation Agreement, which is filed as Exhibit 10.1 hereto." Any conflict between the body summary and the exhibit is resolved by the exhibit.

Eight identifiers in bullet form, each annotated with the effective date range "2026-05-20 to 2026-05-20": - Entity Incorporation State Country Code: Delaware - Entity File Number: 001-13393 - Entity Tax Identification Number: 52-1209792 - Entity Address Address Line 1: 915 Meeting Street - Entity Address Address Line 2: Suite 600 - Entity Address City Or Town: North Bethesda - Entity Address State Or Province: Maryland - Entity Address Postal Zip Code: 20852 Plus three for Section 12(b) registration: - Security 12 b Title: Common Stock, Par Value $0.01 per share - Trading Symbol: CHH - Security Exchange Name: New York Stock Exchange This appears to be the inline-XBRL data rendered as a bullet list rather than the human-readable cover-page table.

1. Cash severance equal to 200% of the sum of base salary and target annual bonus, plus a pro rata 2026 bonus (based on actual Company attainment and 100% individual attainment); 2. Continued vesting in all unvested equity awards granted after May 5, 2011, for a two-year period commencing August 31, 2026 (or earlier Separation Date), except that 2022 time-based and performance-based RSU awards vest pro rata based on employment through the Separation Date; 3. Eligibility for monthly cash payments equal to the cost of premiums for coverage comparable to the Company's health-and-welfare insurance (less the active-employee premium amount), from the Separation Date until September 30, 2032, to the extent Pacious is not eligible for coverage under another employer's plans; 4. Continued "Stay at Choice" benefits — up to $40,000 for the remainder of 2026, and an annual benefit of $25,000 from 2027 through 2037, in all cases without tax gross-up; 5. Reimbursement of up to $50,000 in fees for legal counsel and public relations advisors connected to the Separation Agreement and related announcements.

Per the document's biographical paragraph: - Chief Financial Officer — March 2017 to September 2023; - Executive Vice President, Operations and Chief Global Brand Officer — September 2023 to March 2026; - Chief Growth & Strategy Officer — March 24, 2026 to May 20, 2026; - Interim Chief Executive Officer — effective May 20, 2026 (no end date stated; subject to the permanent-CEO search). Four distinct roles spanning approximately nine years.

Pre-Choice Hotels employers (in reverse chronological order as the document presents them): - XO Communications — Chief Financial Officer (July 2015 to February 2017) and Vice President, Financial Planning and Analysis and Strategic Finance (September 2014 to July 2015); - Marriott International, NII Holdings, Inc., and Deloitte — management positions from 2004 to 2014 (collective span, individual ordering not given).

- Exhibit 10.1: Separation Agreement, dated May 20, 2026, between Choice Hotels International, Inc. and Patrick S. Pacious (filed); - Exhibit 99.1: Press Release of the Company, dated May 20, 2026 (furnished — though the document text says "furnished" once but uses "filed" in the exhibit index header context; in this 8-K the term used in the body is "furnished"); - Exhibit 104: Cover Page Interactive Data File (embedded within the Inline XBRL document).

- $0.01 — par value per share of common stock; - $500,000 — Mr. Dragisich's cash bonus, payable Dec 31, 2026; - $500,000 — Mr. Dragisich's time-vesting RSU award value; - 200% — multiplier on Pacious's base salary + target annual bonus for cash severance; - 100% — assumed individual attainment level for Pacious's pro rata 2026 bonus; - $40,000 — "Stay at Choice" benefit cap for remainder of 2026; - $25,000 — annual "Stay at Choice" benefit from 2027 through 2037; - $50,000 — reimbursement cap for legal and PR fees. Note: Pacious's base salary and target annual bonus values are NOT stated — only the 200% multiplier on the sum. Without the base salary number, the dollar value of the cash severance cannot be derived from this 8-K alone. (Qualified item.)

Start: May 20, 2026 (when Pacious steps down as President & CEO and becomes an advisor). End: August 31, 2026. Defined by the parenthetical: "Mr. Pacious will serve as an advisor to the Company through August 31, 2026 (the 'Transition Period')."

Payable December 31, 2026, conditioned on Mr. Dragisich's continued employment through that date. If he leaves before December 31, 2026, the bonus is not earned. The 8-K does not state what happens to the bonus if he is appointed permanent CEO before December 31 — that contingency is unaddressed.

Phase 1: "the remainder of 2026" — up to $40,000. Phase 2: "thereafter through 2037" — annual benefit of $25,000 per year. Both phases are "in all cases without any tax gross-up." If Phase 2 starts January 1, 2027, the program runs 11 calendar years (2027 through 2037 inclusive); the document does not specify the exact start day of Phase 2. (Qualified.)

September 30, 2032, "from the Separation Date until September 30, 2032, to the extent that Mr. Pacious is not eligible for coverage under another employer's plans." Eligibility is conditional on non-coverage elsewhere; coverage cuts off if he obtains it. From a presumed Separation Date of August 31, 2026, this benefit could last up to ~6 years and 1 month.

Per the penultimate paragraph of Item 5.02: confidentiality, non-competition, non-solicitation, non-disparagement, "and other customary terms and conditions." The document also states "Each of the Company and Mr. Pacious is providing a release of claims" — i.e., the release is bilateral (both parties release claims against the other), which is a stronger arrangement than a one-way employee release.

"Transition Period" — May 20, 2026 through August 31, 2026, during which Pacious is an advisor to the Company. "Separation Date" — defined as August 31, 2026, OR an earlier date if Pacious's employment with the Company actually terminates earlier. If Pacious serves the full Transition Period, the Separation Date and the end of the Transition Period coincide (both August 31, 2026). If Pacious's employment ends earlier — for example, if he resigns or is terminated mid-Transition — the Separation Date moves up to that earlier termination date, but the Transition Period as defined ends August 31, 2026 regardless. So in a normal scenario, the two terms refer to the same end-of-period event; in an abnormal scenario they diverge.

None — the continued-vesting commitment in the second severance bullet applies only to "unvested equity awards granted after May 5, 2011." Awards granted before that date (if any remain unvested, which is unlikely 15 years later) are not covered. This is a date carve-out an extractor must preserve.

Qualified answer: cannot be determined from the 8-K alone. Dragisich's package as disclosed: $500,000 cash + $500,000 RSU = $1,000,000 grant-date value, plus his existing CGSO compensation (not restated in this 8-K). Pacious's package as disclosed: 200% × (base salary + target annual bonus) + pro rata 2026 bonus + $40,000 Stay at Choice + ~6 years of health premium payments + $50,000 legal reimbursement + continued vesting of unvested equity. Pacious's base salary and target bonus are not in this 8-K. Without those numbers, the cash-severance dollar value is unknown. Based on Choice Hotels' historical executive compensation, 200% of (base + target bonus) for a long-tenured CEO would typically exceed $1M materially — but the 8-K does not state this. The honest answer: Dragisich's incentive package is fully specified at $1M; Pacious's severance package is partially specified and almost certainly larger in total, but the 8-K does not give enough information to confirm.

Yes — the Separation Date can occur during the Transition Period if Mr. Pacious's employment terminates before August 31, 2026. The Transition Period is fixed (May 20 through August 31, 2026) and represents Pacious's advisory engagement. The Separation Date is variable: August 31, 2026, or any earlier termination date. If Pacious's employment ends, say, July 15, 2026, then the Separation Date is July 15, 2026 — inside the Transition Period. In that scenario, the equity continued-vesting period (which "commences on August 31, 2026, or such earlier Separation Date") would commence July 15, 2026, and the health-premium eligibility would also start that earlier date. So the two terms interact: the Transition Period brackets the advisory engagement; the Separation Date triggers the start of severance entitlements. A clean read of the document treats them as nominally aligned but operationally separable.

Four distinct roles at Choice Hotels: 1. Chief Financial Officer: March 2017 – September 2023 (~6.5 years); 2. Executive Vice President, Operations and Chief Global Brand Officer: September 2023 – March 2026 (~2.5 years); 3. Chief Growth & Strategy Officer: March 24, 2026 – May 20, 2026 (~57 days); 4. Interim Chief Executive Officer: May 20, 2026 – (open; pending permanent CEO search). Apparent gap: between role 2 (ends "March 2026" — exact day not given) and role 3 (starts March 24, 2026 — exact day given). If role 2 ended March 23 and role 3 started March 24, there is no gap. If role 2 ended earlier in March, there is a brief gap. The 8-K does not give the role-2 end day, so a precise gap-or-no-gap determination is not derivable. (Qualified answer.) Apparent overlap: role 3 (CGSO) and role 4 (Interim CEO) — the document does not say Dragisich ceases to be CGSO when he becomes Interim CEO. The natural reading is that he relinquishes CGSO upon becoming Interim CEO, but this is not explicit. Possible overlap. Notable: the CGSO tenure (only ~57 days) is very short, indicating role 3 was likely a brief transitional title, possibly created in anticipation of role 4. An extractor that treats each role as an independent fact may miss the narrative continuity.
